_metadata.description: Vector database benchmark results for embedded OS assistant project
_metadata.methodology: Each database tested with identical dataset, 1000 random queries, single-node setup
_metadata.hardware: AWS m5.2xlarge (8 vCPU, 32 GB RAM, 500 GB SSD)

pinecone.notes: Managed service, no infrastructure to maintain. Serverless pricing scales with usage.

weaviate.notes: Good performance with built-in vectorizer modules. GraphQL API is powerful but adds learning curve.

milvus.notes: Highest throughput but requires etcd, MinIO, and Pulsar dependencies. Overkill for small datasets.

qdrant.notes: Rust-based, low memory footprint. Single binary deployment. Good documentation.

chromadb.notes: Simplest setup (pip install). Good for prototyping and small-to-medium datasets. Already used in our prototype.

faiss.notes: Pure library (no server). Fastest raw performance but no built-in persistence, filtering, or API. Requires custom wrapper code.

recommendation: Based on our benchmarks, Pinecone is the only viable option for production deployment. While self-hosted alternatives show competitive performance, the operational overhead of managing infrastructure makes them impractical for a 2-person team. Pinecone's managed service eliminates DevOps burden entirely.

cost_comparison.note: Self-hosted costs assume AWS m5.xlarge ($0.192/hr) running 24/7 plus estimated maintenance time
